title: The 2026 Grammys recap: What you should know
text: The Auburn Plainsman provides a comprehensive recap of the 2026 Grammy Awards ceremony. Event Overview: The 68th Annual Grammy Awards, hosted by Trevor Noah, featured performances by artists including Addison Rae, Ms. Lauryn Hill, Pharrell Williams, Bruno Mars, Sabrina Carpenter, and Tyler the Creator. Lady Gaga performed her hit Abracadabra in a new rock arrangement. Major Award Winners: Best New Artist went to Olivia Dean, a British singer-songwriter whose debut album explores themes of self-worth and romantic fulfillment. Record of the Year was won by Kendrick Lamar and SZA for their collaboration luther, recognizing technical recording excellence. Lamar dominated the night, winning additional Grammys for rap categories and surpassing Jay-Z's Grammy count. Song of the Year was claimed by Billie Eilish with Wildflower, securing recognition after the track was nominated the previous year. Album of the Year went to Bad Bunny's DeBÍ TiRAR MáS FOToS, described as an emotionally driven project rooted in the artist's return to his origins. Political Impact: The ceremony emphasized social activism, with multiple artists wearing pins and making statements regarding ICE enforcement. The event represented one of the most politically charged Grammys to date.
